subject: Ecology
title: Scent Signals
description: A short lesson to help you apply Scent Signals.

hook.text: A rose's perfume isn't for you—it's a chemical shout aimed at pollinators.

buildup.text: Flowers release volatile compounds that travel through air to attract specific visitors.

discovery.text: Night-blooming flowers produce strong scents to guide moths that can't rely on color in the dark.

twist.text: Corpse flowers smell like rotting flesh to attract carrion flies that act as pollinators.

climax.text: Each flower's scent blend is a chemical fingerprint that matches its target pollinator.

punchline.text: Flowers don't smell good for us. They smell right for their pollinator.

quiz.question: Why do corpse flowers smell like rotting flesh?
options: To attract carrion flies as pollinators
